summary: CleanMyMac's landing page features strong visual branding with a clear value proposition and prominent CTAs. The page effectively communicates core benefits but lacks social proof above the fold and has some minor usability concerns with the cookie banner placement.
positives: Clear, benefit-driven headline that immediately communicates value ('A Mac cleaner? A Mac saver.')
Strong visual hierarchy with prominent app icon creating brand recognition
Dual CTA strategy with 'Try 7 Days Free' (primary/green) and 'Buy Now' (secondary/outlined) reduces commitment anxiety
30-day money back guarantee displayed near CTAs builds trust
Clean, uncluttered design with good use of whitespace
Consistent branding with pink/purple gradient creating memorable aesthetic

issues.issue: No social proof visible above the fold - missing user counts, ratings, or testimonials
issues.recommendation: Add App Store rating, number of downloads, or brief testimonial quote near the CTAs

issues.issue: Cookie consent banner obscures bottom content and creates visual clutter
issues.recommendation: Use a less intrusive cookie banner design, possibly a smaller floating element or slide-in from corner

issues.issue: Navigation is minimal with only FAQ and Blog - no clear path to learn more about features
issues.recommendation: Add 'Features' or 'How it works' link in navigation for users who need more information before converting

issues.issue: The '30' in the money-back guarantee appears as a separate element/badge that may not be immediately clear
issues.recommendation: Display '30-day money back guarantee' as continuous text for better readability

friction_points: Users may hesitate without seeing reviews or social proof from other Mac users
No pricing information visible - users don't know cost before clicking 'Buy Now'
Lack of feature details may cause information-seeking users to bounce
No indication of what happens after the 7-day trial (auto-billing?)
best_practices_missing: Trust badges (App Store badge, security certifications, press logos)
Pricing transparency or at least a hint at cost
Feature preview or screenshot of the actual product interface
Clear explanation of trial terms (what happens after 7 days)
quick_wins: Add App Store rating badge (e.g., '4.8 stars from 50,000+ reviews') near CTAs
Include 'As featured in' press logo strip below the fold
Add micro-copy under 'Try 7 Days Free' clarifying 'No credit card required' if applicable
Make the 30-day guarantee more prominent with a small icon/checkmark
